2605.16806.title: Cross-modal Affinity-aligned Multimodal Learning Analytics for Predicting Student Collaboration Satisfaction in Game-Based Learning
2605.16806.paper_summary: Collaborative game-based learning environments offer rich opportunities for small-group knowledge construction, yet automatically predicting student collaboration satisfaction remains challenging. A critical barrier is modality degradation: in educational deployments, individual modalities such as eye gaze exhibit inconsistent informativeness across student cohorts, causing implicit attention-based fusion to produce brittle multimodal representations. We propose the Affinity-Aligned Multimodal Learning Analytics (AAMLA) framework, whose core contribution is the Cross-modal Affinity-guided Modality Alignment (CAMA) module, which explicitly models inter-modal relationships via affinity matrices and enforces cross-modal consistency through contrastive learning, enabling adaptive suppression of uninformative modalities without discarding them. AAMLA further applies modality-specific projection layers to map heterogeneous features, including facial action units, head pose, eye gaze, and interaction trace logs, into a unified semantic space prior to alignment. Experiments on 50 middle school students in the EcoJourneys collaborative learning environment demonstrate consistent improvements over unimodal baselines and prior cross-attention approaches under standard and modality degradation conditions, with SHAP and t-SNE analyses confirming that CAMA produces robust, interpretable cross-modal representations for student collaboration modeling.
2605.16806.paper_summary_zh: 協作遊戲式學習環境為小組知識建構提供了豐富的機會，但自動預測學生的協作滿意度仍然具有挑戰性。一個關鍵的障礙是模態降級：在教育部署中，個別模態如眼球注視在不同學生群體中表現出不一致的信息性，導致基於隱式注意力的融合產生脆弱的多模態表示。我們提出了親和力對齊多模態學習分析（AAMLA）框架，其核心貢獻是跨模態親和力引導的模態對齊（CAMA）模塊，該模塊通過親和力矩陣明確建模模態間的關係，並通過對比學習強化跨模態一致性，實現自適應抑制無信息模態而不丟棄它們。AAMLA進一步應用特定模態的投影層，將異質特徵（包括面部動作單元、頭部姿勢、眼球注視和互動追蹤日誌）映射到對齊前的統一語義空間。對50名中學生在EcoJourneys協作學習環境中的實驗顯示，在標準和模態降級條件下，相較於單模態基準和之前的跨注意力方法，AAMLA在性能上持續改善，SHAP和t-SNE分析確認CAMA為學生協作建模產生了穩健且可解釋的跨模態表示。
2605.16806.author: Wen-Hsin Tsai et.al.
2605.16806.authors: Wen-Hsin Tsai, Chia-Ming Lee, Yuk-Ying Tung
